description: Allow users to create new blog posts via a form in the frontend, with data stored in-memory on the backend

tasks.task: Add a POST endpoint to create new blog posts. In src/blog/blog.service.ts, add a `create(data: { title: string, body: string }): Post` method that generates a new id (use `Math.max(...this.posts.map(p => p.id), 0) + 1`), sets createdAt to new Date(), adds the post to the in-memory `this.posts` array, and returns the created post. In src/blog/blog.controller.ts, add a `@Post()` endpoint decorated with `@Body()` that calls `this.blogService.create(data)`. Import `Post` and `Body` from '@nestjs/common'. Also add CORS support if not present - in src/main.ts add `app.enableCors()` before the listen call.

Create a CreatePost form component and integrate it into the app. Create src/components/CreatePost.tsx with a form containing title (input) and body (textarea) fields, and a submit button. On submit, POST to 'http://localhost:3000/posts' with JSON body { title, body }. The component should accept an `onPostCreated: (post: Post) => void` callback prop. In src/App.tsx, add state to toggle between list view and create form (`showCreateForm`), add a 'New Post' button in the PostList view that sets showCreateForm to true, and render CreatePost when showCreateForm is true. When a post is created, call the callback to add it to the local posts state and return to the list view. In src/components/PostList.tsx, modify to accept an optional `posts` prop to allow parent to manage posts state, and add the 'New Post' button. Add appropriate CSS styles for the form in src/style.css (e.g., .create-post-form, form inputs, textarea).

example_backend: POST /posts with valid JSON { title: 'Test', body: 'Content' } returns 201 with created post including id and createdAt
GET /posts returns the newly created post in the list
Created post persists in memory until server restart
example_frontend: Clicking 'New Post' button shows the create post form
Form has title input, body textarea, and submit button
Submitting form with valid data creates post and returns to list view
New post appears in the post list after creation
Form validation prevents submitting with empty title
Cancel/back button returns to list without creating post
